title: Sustained Hits
teaches: a critical hit can multiply: Sustained Hits turns each natural 6 to hit into extra ordinary hits on top of itself.
intro: Your Necron Immortals have set aside their gauss blasters and taken up tesla carbines for this battle — crackling arc-casters that reward lucky shots. Tesla carbines carry SUSTAINED HITS 2: every natural 6 on the hit roll — a critical hit — adds 2 extra ordinary hits to the pool on top of the hit it already scored. A mob of twenty Ork Boyz is charging your line; ten Immortals fire two shots each, so watch the HIT line: whenever sixes come up, the hits counted will be more than the dice that passed.

turns.narrate_before: Fire the first volley. The HIT line will do the arithmetic in the open: hits rolled, plus the sustained bonus from any critical, equals the pool that goes on to wound.

Fire again — and notice the bonus hits are ordinary hits. Only the natural 6 itself is the critical; the two hits it brings along still have to roll to wound (4+ here, Strength 5 against the Boyz' Toughness 5) and the Orks still take their 5+ save against every one.

outro: That is the second ability fed by the same trigger. A natural 6 to hit is a critical hit, and Sustained Hits 2 grows each one into two additional ordinary hits — more dice carried into the wound roll, not automatic wounds. Compare it with Lethal Hits from the last scenario: the same critical, spent differently — Lethal skips the wound roll for that one attack, Sustained multiplies the attack instead. Ask 'why hit' after a volley to read the full rule. One critical-hit ability remains: Devastating Wounds, which turns critical WOUNDS into damage that ignores saves — that one is waiting for a scenario of its own.
